informal: Prove that the power series $\sum nz^n$ does not converge on any point of the unit circle.
lean:
theorem Shakarchi_exercise_1_19a (z : ℂ) (hz : ‖z‖ = 1) (s : ℕ → ℂ)
    (h : s = (λ n => ∑ i ∈ (range n), i * z ^ i)) :
    ¬ ∃ y, Tendsto s atTop (𝓝 y)
reasoning: The Lean statement fixes an arbitrary z ∈ ℂ on the unit circle (‖z‖ = 1) and asserts that the sequence of partial sums s_n = ∑_{i<n} i·z^i has no limit in ℂ, which is precisely the assertion that ∑ n z^n diverges at every point of the unit circle. The quantifier "for any point on the unit circle" is captured by the universally quantified z with hypothesis ‖z‖ = 1, and "does not converge" is captured by ¬∃ y, Tendsto s atTop (𝓝 y). The partial sum encoding via Finset.range n with summand i · z^i correctly represents the series ∑ n z^n. No extra hypotheses are added and none are dropped.
